ADR 0004 scalar-indexed UTF-8 applies. ASCII/direct paths and compiler-built constants precede local helpers and narrow string or Unicode slices.

Ordinary Haxe String.charAt uses ADR 0004 Unicode-scalar indexing and returns a borrowed UTF-8 view of the selected character, or the empty string for a negative or out-of-range index. The focused hosted suite covers ASCII, a non-BMP character, embedded NUL, empty input, generated layouts, runtime planning, strict C11, optimization, and sanitizers; other String methods and environments remain explicit unsupported boundaries.
